ELPAC Level 3
Lexile BR–300L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Two Headlines, One Wall

STS-MS8-L3-BR300-MSELA8-CPO-LAT-01 • MS ELA Grade 8 • Analyze Conflicting Information from Two or More Texts
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
In many Latinx neighborhoods, murals are like giant public storybooks. Painters called muralistas cover whole walls with images of family, history, and hope, a tradition made famous by Mexican artists almost a hundred years ago. But when two newspapers describe the same mural, they do not always agree, and readers become the jury.
Why might two writers report such different facts about the very same wall?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Marisol doing?
Look at the picture. Say: Marisol is ____. (pointing • sitting) Then say: She wants the jury to see the ____. (sign • gavel)
2
What is Diego holding?
Look at the picture. Say: Diego holds a ____. (chart • poster) Then say: It shows what the two articles ____. (say • cost)
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Say: The teacher holds a ____. (scale • book) Then say: She is ____ the two stories. (weighing • reading)
4
What place is this? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Say: This is a ____. (library • store) Then say: I know because I see many ____. (books • cars)
5
Tell me about the evidence table.
Look at the picture. Say: The table has two ____. (posters • hats) Then say: The two signs do not ____. (agree • fit)
6
What is Ximena wearing?
Look at the picture. Say: Ximena wears a ____. (purple sweater • green hoodie) Then say: She sits at the ____ desk. (jury • lunch)
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: CA CCSS RI.8.9 — Analyze conflicting information presented in two or more texts on the same topic. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
